title: Police (Amendment) Act, 2023
text:
No. 2 of 2023
VIRGIN ISLANDS
POLICE (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2023
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title and commencement
2. New section 35A inserted
1

No. 2 of 2023 Police (Amendment) Act, 2023 Virgin
Islands
I ASSENT
(Sgd.) John Rankin CMG,
Governor.
20th March, 2023
VIRGIN ISLANDS
No. 2 of 2023
AN ACT TO AMEND THE POLICE ACT, REVISED EDITION 2013, AND TO PROVIDE FOR
MATTERS CONCERNING THEREWITH.
[Gazetted 21st March, 2023]
ENACTED by the Legislature of the Virgin Islands as follows:
Short title and commencement
1. (1) This Act may be cited as the Police (Amendment) Act, 2023.
(2) This Act shall come into force on such date as the Governor may, by
Proclamation published in the Gazette, appoint.
New section 35A inserted
2. The Police Act, Revised Edition 2013, is amended by inserting
immediately after section 35, the following new section:
“Power to collect intimate samples
35A (1) Except as provided by this section, a member of the Force
shall not take or record an intimate sample from a person without the
consent of that person.
(2) Consent to the taking of an intimate sample by a person
shall be recorded either in writing or in an audio or audio-visual
recording.
(3) Where a person refuses to consent to the taking of his or
her intimate sample under subsection (1), a member of the Force may
take an intimate sample, without consent of such person, subject to an
order providing for an intimate sample to be taken signed by a
Magistrate or a Judge of the High Court.
(4) A member of the Force seeking an order for the taking of an
intimate sample shall apply to A Magistrate or Judge in writing, and
2

shall state the offence being investigated and detail his or her grounds
for believing that:
(a) the person from whom the intimate sample is sought is
involved in an arrestable offence; and
(b) the intimate sample will tend to prove or disprove the
involvement of the person from whom the intimate
sample is sought, in the arrestable offence being
investigated.
(5) A Judge or Magistrate, may only make an order for the taking
of an intimate sample where he or she:
(a) has reasonable grounds for suspecting that the person
from whom the intimate sample is sought is involved in
an arrestable offence; and
(b) is satisfied that the intimate sample will tend to prove or
disprove the involvement of the person from whom the
intimate sample is sought, in the arrestable offence being
investigated.
(6) The reasons for granting the order mentioned in subsection (5)
above shall be contained in writing and shall be disclosed to the person
against whom the order is granted.
(7) Where an order of the Court has been given and it is proposed
that an intimate sample shall be taken in pursuance of the order, a
member of the Force shall inform the person from whom the sample is
to be taken
(a) of the nature of the offence in the commission of which it
is suspected that the person has been involved;
(b) of the order granted by the Court; and
(c) the grounds or reasons for the granting of the Order by the
Court pursuant to subsection (6);
and such matters under paragraphs (a) to (c) shall be recorded
immediately after the sample is taken.
(8) Where an order is granted for the taking of an intimate sample
or where consent is given, in the case of an intimate sample which is a
dental impression, the sample may be taken from a person only by a
dental practitioner.
(9) Where an order is granted for the taking of an intimate sample
or where consent is given, except in the case of a sample of urine or
buccal swab, the sample may be taken from a person only by a
(a) registered medical practitioner; or
(b) a registered nurse.
(10) For the purposes of this Act an intimate sample shall not be
taken from a child under this Act, except
3

(a) where consent has been obtained in writing or in an audio
or audio-visual recording from a parent or guardian of the
child; or
(b) in the absence of such consent under paragraph (a), an
order of the Court authorising the taking of a sample from
the child is obtained in accordance with subsection (5).
(11) In circumstances where a person from whom an intimate
sample is taken
(a) is acquitted;
(b) is not charged; or
(c) is charged, but the prosecution does not proceed with the
charge or the proceedings are discontinued,
the intimate sample taken from him and her and any evidence, records
or documentation connected thereto shall, after the decision is taken
not to charge the said person or upon the discharge or acquittal of the
person, be destroyed in the presence of a Gazetted Officer or Inspector.
(12) The Commissioner shall provide or cause to be provided to
the person to whom subsection (11) applies, written confirmation that
the intimate sample, all evidence, records or documentation in relation
thereto have been destroyed, including confirmation that the facility or
service provider which analyses intimate samples shall also destroy any
such evidence, records or documentation or other such records.
(13) For the avoidance of doubt under subsection (11) and (12),
“documentation” includes analysis, reports and other related items, but
does not include court documents such as warrants or orders.
(14) Nothing in this section applies to any person
(a) arrested or detained under the Counter-Terrorism Act,
2021, No. 33 of 2021; or
(b) arrested under an extradition arrest power.
(15) For the purposes of this section unless the context otherwise
requires
“arrestable offence” means an offence prescribed as such in
Schedule 1 to the Criminal Code, Revised Edition
2013.
“child” has the meaning as assigned under section 2 of the
Children and Young Persons Act, Revised Edition
2013;
“dental practitioner” has the meaning as assigned under
section 2 of the Medical, Dental and Allied Health
Practitioners Act, 2000, No. 4 of 2000;
“guardian” has the meaning as assigned under section 2 of the
Children and Young Persons Act, Revised Edition
2013;
“intimate sample” means
4

(a) a sample of
(i) blood;
(ii) semen;
(iii) any other tissue fluid;
(iv) urine;
(v) saliva; or
(vi) public hair;
(b) a dental impression; or
(c) a swab taken from any part of a person’s genitals or
from a person’s body orifices other than the mouth.
“Medical practitioner” has the meaning as assigned under
section 2 of the Medical, Dental and Allied Health
Practitioners Act, 2000, No. 4 of 2000; and
“nurse” has the meaning as assigned under section 2 of the
Nurses and Midwives Act, 2020, No. 25 of 2020.
Passed by the House of Assembly this 9th day of March, 2023.
(Sgd.) Corine N. George-Massicote,
Speaker.
(Sgd.) Phyllis Evans,
Clerk of the House of Assembly.
5
